A test case to verify the Boolean conversion of a true value in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that verifies the behavior of the `Boolean` function in JavaScript. It checks if the expression `Boolean(true)` evaluates to `true`, ensuring that the `Boolean` function correctly converts the boolean value `true` to a boolean type. This is a narrow functionality test, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion. The code uses the `assert` function to perform the test, which will throw an error if the condition is not met, indicating a failure in the expected behavior.
